description: The entity communicates internal control responsibilities, policies, and reporting paths to personnel through standard and separate communication channels.

subcriteria.name: policy_and_responsibility_distribution
subcriteria.reference_text: Internal control and security policies are distributed to personnel through a maintained internal system such as a wiki, policy portal, HRIS workflow, or onboarding package. Real evidence often mentions policy publication, employee access to procedures, policy announcements, and training or acknowledgment requirements.

subcriteria.name: standard_personnel_channels
subcriteria.reference_text: The document identifies standard communication channels employees use to receive updates or report issues related to controls. Real evidence often mentions Slack channels, company all-hands, internal mailing lists, wiki pages, team announcements, or email aliases such as security@company.com.

subcriteria.name: confidential_reporting_and_escalation
subcriteria.reference_text: A strong internal communication artifact shows separate or confidential paths for raising concerns when normal channels are not appropriate. Real evidence often mentions an anonymous hotline, whistleblower channel, ethics platform, incident-reporting path, or escalation procedures posted for employees.

subcriteria.name: management_and_board_visibility
subcriteria.reference_text: Some internal communication artifacts also show how control information flows upward to management and the board. Real evidence may mention security briefings, monthly leadership review of metrics, or board minutes documenting risk, audit, or investment decisions.

mismatch_signals.reason: Technical security or resilience evidence is not internal communication evidence.
